title: 2026 Golden Globes Best Actress, Comedy: Rose Byrne Wins (I Had Legs)
text: Rose Byrne Wins 2026 Golden Globe for Best Actress in Musical or Comedy. Australian actress Rose Byrne claimed her first-ever Golden Globe at the 2026 awards ceremony for her role in the A24 film 'If I Had Legs I'd Kick You'. Byrne appeared genuinely surprised upon hearing her name announced. She delivered an unprepared acceptance speech, telling the audience: 'Of course, I didn't prepare anything.' She credited the film's writer-director Mary Bronstein and humorously noted the low-budget production, describing it as a project where 'We shot this movie in 25 days for like $8.50.' She explained that her longtime partner Bobby Cannavale missed the ceremony due to a personal errand. She revealed that 'he went to a reptile expo in New Jersey' to purchase a bearded dragon for their family. Byrne thanked her brother George for accompanying her to the Beverly Hilton event and mentioned that her parents 'bought Paramount+ so they could watch the Golden Globes from Sydney.' Byrne defeated notable nominees including Cynthia Erivo ('Wicked: For Good'), Kate Hudson ('Song Sung Blue'), Amanda Seyfried ('The Testament of Ann Lee'), and Emma Stone ('Bugonia'). The ceremony was hosted by Nikki Glaser and aired on CBS and Paramount+ With Showtime.
